title: Anthropic maps how Claude’s expressed values vary by model and language
friendlyTitle: Anthropic studies how Claude behaves in different languages
summary: Anthropic analyzed 309,815 anonymized Claude conversations and condensed variation in expressed values into four axes: deference versus caution, warmth versus rigor, depth versus brevity, and candor versus execution. It found meaningful differences across model versions and among the 20 most-used Claude languages.
friendlySummary: Anthropic studied many Claude chats and found that Claude’s style can change between model versions and languages, such as being warmer or more focused on detail.
whyItMatters: For people deploying assistants globally, behaviour is not uniform simply because the underlying product is. The work offers a concrete way to test conversational character alongside accuracy and safety.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can help people check whether an AI assistant behaves in a suitable and consistent way for different users.
